title: Evening Update - Volatility Spike in Commodities
commentary: Crude oil climbs 2.3% on OPEC+ production discipline, while gold retreats 0.8% as bond yields rise. Tech sectors show divergence: mega-caps consolidate near 52-week highs while AI upstarts face profit-taking. Bitcoin holds above $92k amid renewed institutional interest; USD strength pressures precious metals lower.

title: Asia Session Start
commentary: Overnight Asian markets digest Monday's strong US equity bounce and oil pullback as geopolitical tensions ease on Hormuz reassurance. Bitcoin maintains strength near $74K (+2.9% on day) while Ethereum surges (+9.7%), signaling renewed appetite for risk assets after last week's volatility. Equities poised to extend gains with energy stocks cooling on oil's retreat toward $95, though inflation concerns linger with Fed decision looming midweek. Gold holds near $5,020 as investors balance growth recovery against persistent macro uncertainty.

title: Market Close
commentary: Equity markets bounce sharply into the close as Treasury yields compress and oil retreats to $95 on Bessent's Hormuz reassurance. Dow rallies +400 points (0.9%) while S&P 500 recovers +0.7% after early morning pressure tied to geopolitical tensions. Tech leadership emerges with Nasdaq +1.1% as safe-haven flows reverse and investors rotate back into growth. Gold softens -0.3% amid risk-on sentiment, Bitcoin holds near $73K, while crypto's resilience signals growing conviction that the oil shock may be temporary. Momentum shifts decisively bullish heading into the Fed's Wednesday decision.

title: Mid-Day Market Close
commentary: Equities fade into the close as tech leadership wavers amid persistent rate-cut hesitation. S&P 500 drifts -0.3%, Nasdaq -0.6% on rotation from mega-cap AI to cyclicals. Gold +1.2%, oil -1.8% reflect mixed macro signals. Bitcoin steadies near $62.5k as Fed speakers fuel volatility. Bond volatility creeping higher on resilient inflation data.

title: Late Morning
commentary: Markets grind sideways in late-morning trade with S&P 500 hovering near flat as last week's relief rally stalls. Tech consolidates gains with NVIDIA off 0.3% and Apple treading water near unchanged. Oil stabilizes around $92 after Monday's sharp pullback from Hormuz tensions easing, while gold holds firm at $5,020 as investors balance risk-on sentiment against lingering geopolitical concerns. Bitcoin trades choppy near $74.5K as crypto's recent outperformance attracts profit-taking. Volume light ahead of Wednesday's Fed decision.

title: Morning Rally
commentary: Bulls charge out of the gate with Dow surging 575 points as oil craters to $93—down over 5% intraday—on Bessent's Hormuz reassurance. Tech leading the charge with NVIDIA and Meta rallying, pushing Nasdaq up 1.4% while small caps outperform via Russell 2000's 1.6% gain. Crypto catches the risk-on bid with Ethereum popping nearly 5% and Bitcoin breaking $74K. Gold holds steady at $5,030 as investors rotate from safety plays back into equities.

title: Market Open
commentary: Wall Street kicks off the week with relief as oil retreats to $95 after Bessent confirms US allowing Iranian tankers through Hormuz. Dow futures jumped 350 points overnight on hopes the shipping chokepoint impasse may ease, while crypto continues its defiant run with Bitcoin back above $73K. Gold holds steady near $5,000 but silver lags—investors rotating back into risk after last week's brutal selloff. All eyes on the Fed this week with the oil-driven inflation spike complicating the rate outlook.

title: Asia Session
commentary: Asia-Pacific markets tumble as investors brace for a prolonged Middle East conflict—Nikkei and KOSPI both deep in the red. Oil holds near $100 as Strait of Hormuz fears persist; Bessent announces US will allow temporary purchases of stranded Russian crude to stabilize energy markets. Gold rallies as safe-haven flows intensify while defense stocks remain in focus. The specter of 1970s-style stagflation looms large with the Iran war now expected to drag on for months.

title: Final Hour
commentary: Markets attempt a bounce in the final hour with S&P 500 clawing back to -0.31% from session lows near -0.6%. Short-covering and weekend positioning help lift the tape, though tech remains under pressure with Apple down 1.8% and NVIDIA shedding 1.2%. Bitcoin stabilizes at $71K—Bloomberg notes it's nearing zones where past bear markets have bottomed. Russell 2000 improves to -0.34% as small caps attract nibbles. Heading into the close with losses but well off the day's worst levels.

title: Late Afternoon
commentary: Selling accelerates into the final stretch with S&P 500 down 0.55% as fund managers square up before the weekend. NVIDIA plunges 1.4% and Apple slides 1.8% in a brutal day for megacap tech. Bitcoin surrenders earlier gains, fading from $72K to $71K as crypto correlations tick higher with equities. Russell 2000 underperforms at -0.72%, signaling broad risk aversion beyond just big tech. Gold steadies at session lows while Treasuries continue their slow bleed.
